slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
slides.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
slides.speaker_notes: Export kind: policy_contract_comparison_deck. Deck family: comparison. Sources: structured_result, document_agent_comparison.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
- The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) documents synthetic evidence artifacts for the Q1 2024 Northwind vendor-control review, with critical gaps (e.g., missing approval email in EV-002) directly blocking control closure and audit sign-off, while the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a separate, narrowly scoped access control mechanism for two administrators with strict operational and quarterly oversight requirements.  
- The Evidence Log focuses on resolving incomplete evidence to enable audit compliance, whereas the Approval Email enforces ongoing governance through quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation to prevent unauthorized privileged access.  
- The Evidence Log’s synthetic artifacts require explicit provenance tracking to avoid misinterpretation of placeholder data, while the Approval Email’s conditions (e.g., ticketed support scope, break-fix limitations) inherently contain access scope to mitigate risk.  
- Control closure in the Evidence Log is contingent on resolving missing evidence items, whereas the Approval Email’s access is inherently time-bound and requires revalidation for personnel changes, introducing continuous oversight rather than a one-time remediation task.  
- The Evidence Log’s gaps are technical and procedural (e.g., undated/unsigned evidence), while the Approval Email’s risks are operational (e.g., unauthorized use outside defined tasks), demanding different mitigation approaches.  
- The Evidence Log’s synthetic nature necessitates careful documentation to prevent audit misinterpretation, whereas the Approval Email’s conditions are explicitly designed to limit exposure through defined workflows and vendor accountability.  
- Recommendation: Resolve all Evidence Log gaps (e.g., complete EV-002 approval email, clarify EV-003 status) before audit sign-off to avoid control failure, while maintaining the Approval Email’s governance framework as a separate, ongoing control to sustain privileged access oversight.
